name: Explainable Scoring Baseline
principles: Only live evidence can be scored.
Missing, stale, candidate, scaffold, and display-only rows do not silently become neutral.
Each asset class has its own directional map and relevance weight.
U.S. macro evidence is not treated as equally relevant to every global asset.
Every asset score must be auditable from counted rows, excluded rows, drivers, conflicts, and confidence.

live_scored.description: Fresh/live row with direct or meaningful asset relevance.

live_context.description: Fresh/live row that informs interpretation but is too indirect to move the score in this pass.

display_only.description: Visible row for user awareness only.

not_live.description: Missing, stale, candidate, scaffold, or unverified row; cannot score.

insufficient.description: Not enough live direct evidence to make a meaningful pressure read.

conflict_rules.definition: Conflict is the smaller absolute side divided by total absolute scored pressure. A high value means meaningful positive and negative evidence coexist.
